meta_notes: Both feeds rebuilt clean this morning, but the underlying Apify extract is still 2026-07-17 (2.0d old) — same source snapshot as yesterday, so ages and views are 2 days behind live. News scan ran: no dermatology story passed the ≤7d test — the bemotrizinol/FDA coverage dates to the June 9 final order, and the DermaRite Burkholderia recall traces to July 2025, so both were dropped rather than dated from CMS metadata.

themes.name: Sun protection is a habit, not a serum

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. 👀 Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade. This is what dermatol

videos.author: drspf
videos.caption: The sun shirt kids were right all along. 🤙 Sun protection is smart, evidence-based, and anti-aging- let’s not go backwards in our thinking since WE KN

videos.author: drabby6
videos.caption: 1. Sunscreen doesn’t cause cancer. The myth that sunscreen causes cancer evolves from evidence that some chemical sunscreens get absorbed through the

her_coverage.summary: Sunscreen-myth video 4.8d old; 30 transcripts touch sun protection — but only ONE names UPF clothing out loud

themes.corpus_position: “You still need broad spectrum SPF, a hat, UPF clothing, and if you want bonus protection you can add polypodium.”

angles.angle: name the habit stack: hat, shade, UPF, reapply
answer ‘sunscreen causes cancer’ with the habit stack
show a Mohs defect, then name the habit that prevents it

her_wins.open_lane: glass-skin part 2: the pregnancy-safe version — she already flagged ‘no retinoid when pregnant’ in the caption and never made the follow-up

her_wins.summary: ♥ follower magnet — pricey skin & hair picks worth the money: 580K (TT) in 2.6d, 24.7× baseline, ♥ top 8%. She is inside today’s hottest derm format and pacing with derms far bigger than her.
her_wins.open_lane: part 2 — the SAVE list: drugstore picks that match the splurge (her 2022 corpus position already says they do)

her_wins.summary: Sunscreen-doesn’t-cause-cancer myth-bust: 180K (TT) in 4.8d, 7.7× her own baseline — a quiet breakout that lands right inside today’s sun-habit wave.
her_wins.open_lane: follow it with the habit stack — hat, shade, UPF shirt — while the drspf sun-shirt wave is live

stitch_shortlist.author: @bbyambi
stitch_shortlist.claim: expensive things that are actually worth the money

rubric.misses: 4.7d old — past the <4d rising window, but still pacing 410K views/day

pitch.the_claim: A civilian ranks her pricey buys as ‘actually worth it’ — skincare sitting alongside handbags and gadgets.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.93M in 4.7d at 410K/day, and it is the exact format she is already winning with this week (her own pricey-picks video: 580K in 2.6d, ♥ top 8%). Stitching it puts a dermatologist inside a wave she is already trending in.
pitch.your_fit: Corpus-grounded and slightly against the grain — her 2022 money guide says ‘lots of things you can get just at the drugstore do as good of a job as the very more expensive well advertised products.’ Her own splurge list is 2.6d old, so the repeat angle is the SAVE half, not the splurge half.
angles: ‘yes, and’ — keep two of hers, swap the rest for drugstore
empathy-first: I own the expensive one, here is when it matters
‘you want me to WHAT?’ — the one on her list I would never buy
pitch.length: original eats ~8s; 25–40s total — her own winner in this format ran 19s

pitch.the_claim: Use zinc oxide as a DIY mask — a sunscreen filter repurposed as a leave-on skincare step, credited to a friend’s ‘tip.’
pitch.why_it_stitches: 6/6. 1.12M in 3.7d and still rising, a specific demonstrable claim, and squarely her lane — zinc oxide is a sunscreen active, not a treatment mask. Punches sideways at a hack, not at a person.
pitch.your_fit: Fully grounded. She has 30 transcripts on sunscreen selection including ‘all of these are available at a drugstore and you’re going to put that on every day no matter what the UV index is’ — she can explain what zinc oxide actually does on skin without any new position.
angles: empathy-first: zinc oxide is great — as sunscreen, not a mask
myth-bust: what a filter does vs what a mask does
‘switch, don’t quit’ — the actual product for that goal

stitch_shortlist.author: @the.art.of
stitch_shortlist.claim: chemical SPF 50 evangelism — ‘SPF stan til I die’

rubric.misses: 5.7d — outside the rising window; included on comment heat (1,064) and exact lane fit

pitch.the_claim: A civilian loudly defends chemical SPF 50 — and the comments are fighting about whether chemical sunscreen is safe.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.02M views with 1,064 comments: the controversy is in the replies, not the video. This is the sunscreen-safety fight playing out without a dermatologist in it, and she published the exact rebuttal 4.8 days ago.
pitch.your_fit: Directly covered — her sunscreen-myth video is 4.8d old (180K, 7.7× baseline) and her corpus position adds the layer she can own: ‘you still need broad spectrum SPF, a hat, UPF clothing.’ A repeat needs the NEW angle: chemical vs mineral is the wrong argument.
angles: ‘yes, and’ — she’s right, and here’s the part everyone skips
settle it: chemical vs mineral is not the real question
empathy-first: the comments are scared, here’s the data

stitch_shortlist.author: @lucy_seitz
stitch_shortlist.claim: cut my hair off because of heat damage

rubric.misses: 5.5d and only 71 comments — low controversy; surfaced on size and on hair being her underexploited lane

pitch.the_claim: Heat damage was bad enough that the only fix was cutting it off — framed as a healthy-hair reset.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.46M in 5.5d at 265K/day, and hair is the high-ceiling lane she under-serves. The half-right trigger is real: cutting removes damaged hair, it does not treat the scalp or the cause.
pitch.your_fit: Partially grounded — she has hair-loss corpus (telogen effluvium, minoxidil-applied-wrong, March 2026) but nothing on heat/mechanical damage specifically. The scalp-and-cause layer is hers to add; the cosmetic claim itself needs her take.
angles: ‘yes, and’ — the cut fixes the shaft, not the scalp
empathy-first: damaged hair does not repair, it grows out
the three heat rules that save the next six inches

rubric.misses: 367K — smallest on the list; earns the slot on claim quality, not reach

pitch.the_claim: Brew clove water and put it on your face — ‘natural antibacterial’ for glowing skin.
pitch.why_it_stitches: The cleanest myth-bust on the board: a specific, repeatable, at-home recipe with a mechanism claim attached. Eugenol is a well-known contact allergen — this is the DIY that sends people to her clinic.
pitch.your_fit: Grounded in pattern, not in a clove-specific quote — she has the natural-remedy lane on tape (‘how to treat nail fungus naturally’) and the credibility to say when natural is fine and when it burns. Confirm she wants to name eugenol on camera.
angles: empathy-first: I get the appeal — here is what it does to a barrier
‘switch, don’t quit’ — the actual glow ingredient that works
patch-test rule for every kitchen DIY

stitch_shortlist.author: @drjessicaburgy
stitch_shortlist.claim: nail melanoma is a dark BAND, not a blotch

rubric.misses: 354K and a fellow derm being CORRECT — only works as ‘yes, and’, never as a correction

pitch.the_claim: A dark band running the length of the nail is the melanoma pattern; a blotch is more often a bruise.
pitch.why_it_stitches: Freshest in-lane derm claim on the board (2.7d, still rising) and it feeds the quiz theme directly — nails are the blind spot in every ‘is it cancer?’ series running right now. She is a Mohs surgeon; this is her operating table.
pitch.your_fit: Her skin-cancer corpus is deep (493K BCC guide, melanoma Q&A) but nail-unit melanoma is thin — the Hutchinson sign and the biopsy conversation are hers to add on top of a colleague who is already right.
angles: ‘yes, and’ — the sign she didn’t name: pigment on the cuticle
what actually happens when we biopsy a nail
quiz variant: band, blotch, or bruise?

more_stitches.author: @dr.taha.karan
more_stitches.claim: all retinoids deliver long-term anti-aging efficacy (Turkish-language)

rubric.misses: Turkish-language and 6.7d old — caption translated, claim not verified on camera

pitch.the_claim: All retinoids reach the same anti-aging endpoint over the long run — potency only changes the speed.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.45M at 365K/day, and it is a claim she has already made in her own words: ‘the difference between the retinoids is how fast onset they have.’ Language barrier is the only reason it isn’t in the shortlist — watch before committing.
pitch.your_fit: Corpus-grounded on the retinoid-ladder position; the Turkish audio needs a watch-first check.
angles: ‘yes, and’ — same destination, different tolerability road
the retinoid ladder, ranked by irritation

more_stitches.author: @aqeelaaz_
more_stitches.claim: content unidentifiable from feed (no caption) — watch in-app first

more_stitches.author: @scottoscotto
more_stitches.claim: braids on straight/fine hair — style, not damage

rubric.misses: no explicit claim to answer; 6.9d old — surfaced only by the ≥1M guarantee

pitch.the_claim: Braids as a style choice on hair types that aren’t usually braided.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.33M — clears the guarantee. The derm hook is traction, not fashion: tension styles and traction alopecia.
pitch.your_fit: Needs her take — no traction-alopecia position found in the corpus.
angles: traction alopecia: the hairline tells on you first

rubric.misses: relatability content, no claim — surfaced by the ≥1M guarantee at 346K/day

pitch.the_claim: Curly-hair in-group humor with 1,084 comments doing the work.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.24M in 3.6d and climbing. Comment section is where the real haircare questions are — mine it rather than stitch the video.
angles: answer the top comment instead of the video

more_stitches.author: @febisola_iris
more_stitches.claim: hydration + sun-water routine for protected skin

rubric.misses: reads as a brand plug — lane call is borderline; stated rather than dropped

pitch.the_claim: A ‘sorbet cream + sun water’ routine is what keeps skin hydrated and protected.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.23M at 361K/day inside the sun-protection seed. If ‘sun water’ is being sold as protection, that is a real safety claim worth answering.
pitch.your_fit: Grounded — her sunscreen-selection corpus covers what counts as protection and what doesn’t.
angles: what legally counts as SPF — and what doesn’t
hydration is not photoprotection

rubric.misses: brand content, no derm claim; 1,700 comments is the only real signal — surfaced by the ≥1M guarantee

pitch.the_claim: A new salon haircare launch, presented as a reveal.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.18M and 1,700 comments — the demand is for a verdict nobody neutral is giving.
pitch.your_fit: Needs her take on the specific product.
angles: ingredient-list read, live, no brand deal

rubric.misses: under 1M — surfaced on pace (262K/day) alone; no specific claim

pitch.the_claim: Managing humidity-stressed hair and root touch-ups on holiday.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 262K views/day pace clears the guarantee. Scalp-in-summer is her underexploited lane.
pitch.your_fit: Needs her take on dye/scalp in heat.
angles: scalp sunburn is real — the part in your hair
